round2: Round 2 is in the books, and outside of Marc accidentally nailing OTT in 6, it seems the rest of you were actively trying to get zero points. Seriously, Dad and Stephanie, not a single correct series pick? Impressive in its futility.
round3: Anaheim put Minnesota out of their misery in four, and New Jersey finally put Ottawa down in seven. Marc and Mom apparently missed both memos, while everyone else got at least one winner right, even if no one nailed the games.
round4: Well, that was a disaster. Every single one of you picked Anaheim, and every single one of you was wrong. The Devils took it in 7 games, leaving your collective predictions looking about as useful as a goalie with no pads.
round1: Round 1 was a bloodbath for anyone betting on the favorites, as Detroit and Colorado both got unceremoniously dumped – meaning Marc, Derrick, Jaclyn, and Stephanie, your picks are looking particularly rough. Only Chris seemed to have a clue, actually picking the Wild upset, while Jaclyn's bracket is already in the dumpster fire.
overall: The 2003 NHL playoffs are officially in the books, with the New Jersey Devils proving that even the most excruciating hockey can still result in a Stanley Cup. Marc, displaying either uncanny insight or just blind luck, absolutely dominated the pool with 34 points, leaving the rest of the field in his dust. Chrissy and Stephanie battled it out for a distant second and third, while Jaclyn managed to pick seven teams only to correctly guess a measly two game series, a true talent for inaccuracy. Derrick, meanwhile, cemented his reputation as the family's resident hockey savant by finishing dead last with 20 points, proving some people just enjoy contributing to the prize pot. Better luck next year, or perhaps try a different sport.
